eventTitle: The Outer Space Treaty entered into force
rubricJustification: Binding only for its parties and framed around state obligations; it does not supply a complete commercial settlement code.

eventTitle: The Space Liability Convention entered into force
rubricJustification: State-centered international liability rules, not comprehensive private insurance coverage or adjudication for settlement commerce.

eventTitle: A U.S. commercial space law established national resource-utilization rights
rubricJustification: Binding national legislation establishing commercial space-resource utilization rights; implementing framework beyond treaty principle (EGL 6).

rubricJustification: National framework under Luxembourg jurisdiction, not an international resolution of celestial-resource rights.

rubricJustification: Voluntary nonbinding guidance; adoption does not prove implementation, enforcement, or a settlement governance authority.

rubricJustification: Negative program evidence despite assembled hardware; it must not be treated as a lunar prospecting flight or operational resource survey.

rubricJustification: Launch-specific U.S. liability framework with statutory conditions and limits, not general insurance for off-Earth property or inhabitants.

rubricJustification: Published interface definition; it did not by itself certify vehicles, require adoption, or create operating docking capacity.

rubricJustification: Architecture and interoperability specification, not an operational multi-provider lunar network or demonstrated service-level performance.

rubricJustification: Certification for a specific Earth-to-ISS service, not generalized deep-space transport or an independent settlement passenger market.

eventTitle: Falcon Heavy reached orbit with partial booster recovery
rubricJustification: Falcon Heavy reached orbit and recovered two side boosters while the center core was lost; partial-stack reuse caps at L5 (integrated stage prototypes reflown in relevant environment).

eventTitle: Falcon first-stage recovery reached a three-digit operational count
rubricJustification: Repeated first-stage recovery reached a three-digit operational count; first-stage-only reuse earns capped partial credit (L5), not full-vehicle-reuse credit.

eventTitle: An uncrewed Crew Dragon autonomously docked with the ISS
rubricJustification: Uncrewed Crew Dragon executed autonomous rendezvous and docking to the ISS with cargo handoff; integrated cargo-handling/interface prototype in a relevant environment (L5).

eventTitle: Perseverance landed in Jezero Crater
rubricJustification: Modern precision Mars EDL delivered a rover well below 10 t; scored L5 as a subscale integrated relevant-environment landing, not operational 10 t+ delivery.

review.reviewerNote: Mapped 2026-07-13: previously-collected candidate mapped with graded credit per node TRL/EGL anchors.

eventTitle: Orion demonstrated its ascent launch-abort sequence
rubricJustification: Ascent Abort-2 exercised the full-scale launch-abort separation sequence under representative flight conditions (integrated abort/rescue prototype, L5); not yet qualified operational rescue.

eventTitle: BEAM expanded to full size while attached to the ISS
rubricJustification: Expandable habitat module deployed to full size on the ISS; structural/habitat-concept process proof (L3). Orbital periodic-access demo, not a surface-constructed occupied habitat.

review.reviewerNote: Mapped 2026-07-13: previously-collected candidate mapped with graded credit per node TRL/EGL anchors.

eventTitle: The first ISS roll-out solar array was connected to an operating power channel
rubricJustification: Roll-out solar array integrated into an operating power channel; grid conversion/architecture proof (L3). Mature orbital grid upgrade, not a validated planetary surface grid.

review.reviewerNote: Mapped 2026-07-13: previously-collected candidate mapped with graded credit per node TRL/EGL anchors.

eventTitle: NASA launched its first two-way optical communications relay payload
rubricJustification: First two-way optical communications relay payload activated; protocol/network proof (L3). No planetary-surface endpoint or delay/outage-managed settlement service yet.

review.reviewerNote: Mapped 2026-07-13: previously-collected candidate mapped with graded credit per node TRL/EGL anchors.

eventTitle: Lunar Node-1 operated as a navigation beacon on the Moon
rubricJustification: Lunar Node-1 operated as a positioning beacon on the Moon's surface; surface network/protocol proof (L3). Brief uncrewed beacon, not an operational multi-node lunar network.

review.reviewerNote: Mapped 2026-07-13: previously-collected candidate mapped with graded credit per node TRL/EGL anchors.

actor: NASA, Honeybee Robotics, and Firefly Aerospace
eventTitle: Lunar PlanetVac collected and transferred regolith on the Moon
rubricJustification: PlanetVac pneumatically collected and transferred lunar regolith on the surface; critical acquisition/handling proof (L3). No separation or conversion into a useful consumable yet.

review.reviewerNote: Mapped 2026-07-13: previously-collected candidate mapped with graded credit per node TRL/EGL anchors.

eventTitle: Curiosity began routine autonomous laser-target selection with AEGIS
rubricJustification: Routine autonomous laser-target selection operated on Mars in relevant environment (integrated autonomous decision loop in operation, L5). Science targeting only; ground retained rover control.

review.reviewerNote: Mapped 2026-07-13: previously-collected candidate mapped with graded credit per node TRL/EGL anchors.

eventTitle: CADRE rovers completed cooperative autonomy and environmental ground tests
rubricJustification: Multi-robot cooperative autonomy completed integrated ground qualification (approaching integrated system, below flight relevant-environment; L4). Not yet operated on the lunar surface.

review.reviewerNote: Mapped 2026-07-13: previously-collected candidate mapped with graded credit per node TRL/EGL anchors.

eventTitle: Two ISS robotic arms cooperated in hose and inspection operations
rubricJustification: Two ISS robotic arms cooperated in prepared hose and inspection servicing operations (integrated remote servicing, below autonomous; L4). Teleoperated, not autonomous fault recovery.

review.reviewerNote: Mapped 2026-07-13: previously-collected candidate mapped with graded credit per node TRL/EGL anchors.

actor: NASA, Orbital ATK, Sierra Nevada, and SpaceX
eventTitle: NASA selected three providers for the second ISS cargo contract round
rubricJustification: A second competitive cargo-resupply round bound three providers to recurring ISS logistics revenue; revenue mechanism operating repeatedly across a multi-provider market (L5).

actor: Axiom Space, SpaceX, NASA, and ISS partners
eventTitle: The first private astronaut mission to the ISS completed safely
rubricJustification: First privately organized and paid astronaut mission to the ISS opened a commercial human transport transaction; revenue-producing service for one bounded market (L5).

review.reviewerNote: Mapped 2026-07-13: previously-collected candidate mapped with graded credit per node TRL/EGL anchors.

eventTitle: Eight countries signed the founding Artemis Accords
rubricJustification: Eight founding states signed the Artemis Accords; formal draft instrument of nonbinding principles (L3), not a binding treaty, regulator, or enforcement mechanism.

eventTitle: Artemis Accords participation reached fifty signatory countries
rubricJustification: Artemis Accords participation broadened to fifty signatories; widening adoption of the nonbinding framework (L4), still short of an adopted legally effective settlement-governance regime.

review.reviewerNote: Mapped 2026-07-13: previously-collected candidate mapped with graded credit per node TRL/EGL anchors.

eventTitle: NASA awarded small contracts to purchase collected lunar material
rubricJustification: NASA awarded contracts to purchase in-situ collected lunar material, establishing a formal space-resource transaction instrument (bounded pilot, L3), not a recurring resource market.

review.reviewerNote: Mapped 2026-07-13: previously-collected candidate mapped with graded credit per node TRL/EGL anchors.

eventTitle: Perseverance landed in Jezero Crater
rubricJustification: One robotic campaign integrated launch, deep-space cruise, and precision EDL through governed interfaces (L3 end-to-end architecture and interface proof). One-way and uncrewed with three of seven elements, so capped below L5 and far below the crewed 26-month L8 campaign.

review.reviewerNote: Mapped 2026-07-13: integration node graded partial credit; 26-month L8 finish line unchanged.

eventTitle: Columbia completed a second orbital mission
rubricJustification: Orbiter reflight demonstrates partial-stack reuse in flight and remains capped at L5 because the launch architecture was not fully reusable.

review.reviewerNote: Second orbiter flight supports capped partial-reuse credit only.

eventTitle: Orbital Express transferred propellant and replaceable modules in orbit
rubricJustification: Autonomous in-orbit fluid transfer exceeded a relevant-environment prototype but did not establish operational mission refueling or recurring service.

review.reviewerNote: Fluid-transfer loop supports L7 without routine-service credit.

eventTitle: Dragon completed the first commercial cargo berthing at the ISS
rubricJustification: The demonstration completed orbital cargo rendezvous, berthing, and station handoff but preceded routine operational service.

review.reviewerNote: Cargo handoff supports L7 demonstration credit.

eventTitle: A second commercial provider began operational station cargo delivery
rubricJustification: An operational resupply mission completed accountable cargo delivery and later waste handoff through an established station interface.

review.reviewerNote: Operational cargo delivery satisfies the L8 logistics anchor.

eventTitle: Jules Verne transferred mission propellant to the ISS
rubricJustification: Automatic transfer of 811 kilograms into the ISS propulsion system satisfies operational refueling at useful quantity without proving routine multi-client service.

review.reviewerNote: Useful-quantity operational transfer supports L8, not L9.

eventTitle: Apollo 17 completed the final Apollo lunar landing mission
rubricJustification: The final Apollo lunar landing mission is the supported program-end transition for the demonstrated lunar surface-ascent line; it preserves L8 and starts dormancy.

review.reviewerNote: Program completion records lifecycle end without reducing the demonstrated level.

eventTitle: Expedition 1 began continuous human occupancy of the ISS
rubricJustification: Continuously crewed off-Earth station has operated integrated life-support, health, and logistics for over two decades; the node's L5 anchor grants a shorter off-Earth integrated demonstration. Frequent bulk resupply means the no-material-resupply clock never ran, so held below L8.

review.reviewerNote: Mapped 2026-07-13: integration node graded partial credit; 26-month L8 finish line unchanged.
